description.component: ArpaElement is the foundational base class for all custom elements in the Arpadroid framework. It extends HTMLElement and provides a rich set of features including declarative templating, content projection via zones, a unified property system, structured child element management, internationalization support, and a well-defined lifecycle with numerous hooks for subclass customization.
description.purpose: ArpaElement eliminates the boilerplate typically required when building web components. It standardizes how custom elements are configured, rendered, updated, and destroyed, allowing developers to focus on component-specific logic rather than repetitive setup. It serves as the single inheritance point for every Arpadroid component.

configuration.overview: ArpaElement uses a configuration object that merges user-provided options with sensible defaults. The config is set during construction and can be extended at any time.
configuration.setConfig: Sets the element's configuration by merging the provided object with getDefaultConfig(). Also computes userConfig, which contains only the properties that differ from defaults.
configuration.getDefaultConfig: Returns the base configuration object. Subclasses override this method, call super.getDefaultConfig() with their own defaults, and the results are deep-merged. Default properties include className, variant, templateContainer, handleContent, and templateTypes.
configuration.addConfig: Deep-merges additional configuration into the existing config at any point after construction.
configuration.getUserConfig: Returns only the configuration properties that differ from the defaults, useful for serialization or debugging.
configuration.keyProperties: Key config properties: className (base CSS class), variant (CSS variant modifier), templateContainer (where templates render, defaults to the element itself), handleContent (whether content projection is active, default true), templateTypes (array of template modes to load, default ['content']), templateVars (variables for template rendering), templateChildren (structured child element definitions), attributes (HTML attributes to apply on render), zoneFilter (function to filter zones), zoneSelector (CSS selector for zone elements), zoneResolverSelector (CSS selector pattern for zone containers).

lifecycle.overview: ArpaElement follows a strict lifecycle: construction, connection to the DOM, rendering, and disconnection. Each phase exposes abstract hooks that subclasses can override to inject custom behavior at precisely the right moment.
lifecycle.constructor: During construction the following hooks run in order: _preInitialize() for early setup before config is applied, setConfig() to merge configuration, _preInitializeContent() to apply initial content and attributes from config, _initializeTemplates() to extract template elements from the DOM, _initializeZones() to initialize the zone tracking system, _initializeContent() to cache the original innerHTML, child nodes, and text content, _initialize() for subclass-specific initialization, and finally getPromise() to create the render-completion promise.
lifecycle.connectedCallback: When the element is inserted into the DOM: pre-render callbacks are executed, onReady() is awaited for async initialization, CSS class names are applied, and _isReady is set to true. On the first connection, initializeProperties() and _onInitialized() run. Then _render() is called, followed by _onConnected() and update().
lifecycle.renderPipeline: The _render() method orchestrates: _preRender() for last-minute adjustments, applying config attributes to the DOM, render() to generate and insert HTML, initializeTemplateNodes() to map rendered child elements to templateNodes, _initializeNodes() for post-render DOM queries, _handleZones() to trigger zone insertion, _onDomReady() when the DOM is fully built, and _onRenderComplete() to finalize by appending cached child nodes, firing onRendered callbacks, resolving the render promise, and calling _onComplete().
lifecycle.disconnectedCallback: When the element is removed from the DOM, all registered unsubscribe functions run and _onDestroy() cleans up zone registrations.
lifecycle.attributeChangedCallback: When an observed attribute changes, update() and _onAttributeChanged() are called, enabling reactive updates.
lifecycle.abstractHooks: Abstract hooks available for subclasses: _preInitialize(), _initialize(), initializeProperties(), _onInitialized(), onReady() (async), _preRender(), render(), _initializeNodes() (async), _onDomReady(), _onComplete(), _onConnected(), _onAttributeChanged(), update(), _onLostZone(), _onPlaceZone(), and _onDestroy().

templates.overview: ArpaElement supports three ways to define templates: inline template strings in the config, HTML template elements in the component's markup, and the templateChildren system for structured child elements. All three can use variable placeholders.
templates.inlineTemplates: Set the template property in the config to a string containing placeholders. During render, renderTemplate() replaces each placeholder with the corresponding value from templateVars or the result of calling getTemplateVars().
templates.templateElements: Place template HTML elements as direct children of the component with a template-type attribute. Supported types are content (replaces innerHTML), prepend (inserted before existing content), append (inserted after existing content), list-item, and view. Templates are extracted during construction and stored in the templates object.
templates.templateVariables: Variables are referenced in templates as placeholders wrapped in curly braces. Values can be static strings, numbers, or functions that receive the component as an argument and return the replacement value. Nested variable references are resolved recursively. The getTemplateVars() method returns the current variable map, and templateVars stores the most recently used variables after each render.
templates.templateAttributes: Template elements can carry HTML attributes that are transferred to the rendered output. Template-specific attributes like template-type are stripped, while all others such as class or id are preserved and applied.

templateChildren.overview: The templateChildren system provides a declarative way to define structured child elements. Each child is configured as an object in the templateChildren config property, keyed by name. Children are rendered as HTML during the template phase and their DOM nodes are tracked for later access and updates.
templateChildren.configShape: Each child accepts: tag (HTML element tag, defaults to div), className (CSS class, auto-generated via BEM if omitted), content (string, HTMLElement, or function returning content), canRender (boolean, string property name, or function controlling visibility), hasZone (whether to add a zone attribute for content projection), zoneName (zone identifier), isContent (marks this child as the content container), attr (object or function returning HTML attributes), propName (property name to check for content), and id (element ID).
templateChildren.rendering: Use renderChild(name, options, attributes) in your template to generate the HTML string for a child. The method resolves attributes, processes template variables in content, and wraps everything in the configured tag. If canRender evaluates to false, an empty string is returned.
templateChildren.updating: After the initial render, use setChild(name, config) to replace a child's configuration and update its DOM node, or editChild(name, config) to merge new options into the existing configuration. Both methods call updateChildNode() to synchronize the DOM.
templateChildren.access: Use getChild(name) to retrieve the live DOM node of a rendered child from the templateNodes map. After render, initializeTemplateNodes() automatically finds each child by its BEM class name and stores the reference.

zones.overview: Zones are ArpaElement's content projection mechanism, similar to slots in Shadow DOM but working in the light DOM. They allow parent components to inject content into named placeholders defined by child components.
zones.defining: Define a zone by placing a zone element with a name attribute inside your component's template or markup. During initialization, zoneMixin() extracts these elements from the DOM and stores them in the component's _zones set.
zones.resolution: When zones are processed via _handleZones(), the system looks for a container element matching the zone's name using the pattern specified by zoneResolverSelector (defaults to an attribute selector like [zone='zoneName']). The search walks up the DOM to find the nearest ArpaElement ancestor that owns such a container.
zones.placement: Once a matching container is found, the zone's child nodes are moved into it. The receiving component's _onPlaceZone() hook fires with a payload containing the zone element, its nodes, the zone name, the target component, and the target container. If no container is found, _onLostZone() fires on the parent, giving it a chance to handle the orphaned content.
zones.registry: Each component maintains a zone-registry attribute listing the names of zones that have been placed into it. Use hasZone(name) to check if a zone exists and getZone(name) to retrieve it.
zones.filtering: Set zoneFilter in the config to a function that receives the set of zones and the component, and returns a filtered set. This allows components to selectively accept or reject zones.

properties.overview: ArpaElement provides a unified property system that abstracts over HTML attributes and the JavaScript config object. Properties are accessed by name, with automatic conversion between dashed-attribute-case and camelCase config keys.
properties.resolution: When you call getProperty(name), the system first checks the element's HTML attributes for a matching dashed-case attribute, then falls back to the camelCase equivalent in the config object. This allows properties to be set declaratively in HTML or programmatically in JavaScript with the same access pattern.
properties.methods: getProperty(name) returns a single property value. hasProperty(name) checks if a property exists in attributes or config. getArrayProperty(name) returns the value as an array, splitting comma-separated attribute strings. getProperties(...names) returns an object of multiple property values. deleteProperty(name) removes a property from both the config and the attribute. deleteProperties(...names) removes multiple properties at once.
properties.hasContent: The hasContent(property) method checks whether a named property has meaningful content by verifying the property exists, has a non-empty value, or has a corresponding zone defined.

i18n.overview: ArpaElement integrates with the @arpadroid/i18n library to provide multi-language text support. Each component sets an i18nKey that serves as the base namespace for all its translation lookups.
i18n.methods: i18n(key, replacements, attributes, base) returns an i18n-text HTML component for the given key under the component's i18nKey namespace. It first checks for an override in the element's HTML attributes or config before falling back to the i18n registry. i18nText(key, replacements, base) returns the plain text translation. getText(key) is a shorthand that returns the raw i18n text for a dotted key path.
i18n.overridePriority: Translation values follow a priority chain: first the HTML attribute (e.g. label-text='Custom'), then the config property (e.g. labelText: 'Custom'), and finally the i18n registry lookup using the full key path.
i18n.ariaLabels: resolveAriaLabel(content) extracts the text content from i18n-text HTML components, enabling accessible aria-label attributes derived from internationalized content.

content.overview: ArpaElement manages content projection for light-DOM child nodes. During construction, the element's original innerHTML and child nodes are cached so they can be re-appended after the template renders.
content.initialization: In _initializeContent(), the element stores its innerHTML as _content, its textContent as _textContent, and a copy of its childNodes as _childNodes. If the config includes a content property, _preInitializeContent() sets the innerHTML before caching.
content.contentNode: getContentNode() returns the element that should receive projected content. If the component has template children, it looks for a child marked with the is-content attribute; otherwise, it returns the element itself. Set handleContent to false in the config to disable content projection entirely.
content.methods: setContent(content, container) updates _content and _childNodes from a string or HTMLElement, then applies them to the given container. addChildNodes(nodes) appends additional nodes to the _childNodes array. getChildElements() returns the cached child nodes filtered to Element and Node instances.

css.overview: ArpaElement provides BEM-compatible CSS class management. A base class name is set via the className config property and serves as the foundation for child element class names.
css.getClassName: getClassName() returns the base class name. Pass a string argument to get a BEM element class: getClassName('icon') returns 'baseClass__icon'. This is used throughout the template children system to auto-generate consistent class names.
css.classNames: During connectedCallback, _addClassNames() combines the className config, any classNames array config, and the existing class attribute into a single deduplicated class string applied to the element.
css.variant: The variant config property provides a semantic modifier that subclasses can use to apply alternative styling. Retrieved via getVariant().

callbacks.overview: ArpaElement supports a callback property system that enables parent-child method binding through string references, allowing parent components to pass behavior to children declaratively.
callbacks.callbackReferences: A property value starting with a colon (e.g. ':handleClick') is treated as a callback reference. The system walks up the DOM to find the nearest ArpaElement ancestor and resolves the named method on it.
callbacks.handleCallbackProperty: handleCallbackProperty(element, propertyName, eventName) reads a callback reference from the named property, resolves it to a function on the parent component, and binds it as a DOM event listener for the specified event. ArpaElement automatically calls this for the on-click property during construction.
callbacks.callCallback: callCallback(callbackName, ...args) retrieves a callback property by name and invokes it with the provided arguments, enabling programmatic invocation of parent-defined behavior.

rendering.overview: ArpaElement's rendering system generates HTML from templates and template children, inserts it into the DOM, and provides hooks and callbacks for post-render logic.
rendering.renderMethod: The render(template) method calls renderTemplate() to process the template string with variables, then sets the result as innerHTML. Subclasses can override render() for custom rendering strategies.
rendering.renderTemplate: renderTemplate(template, vars) processes a template string by replacing variable placeholders with values from the vars object. It updates this.templateVars with the current variables for later reference.
rendering.reRender: reRender() resets the render state (_hasRendered, _isReady), re-extracts templates and zones, creates a new render promise, and re-runs connectedCallback() to perform a full re-render cycle.
rendering.throttling: canRender() implements throttle-based protection against rapid re-renders. It returns false if the element was rendered within the last 200ms, preventing performance issues from cascading updates.
rendering.renderCallbacks: Three callback registration methods are available: onRendered(callback) fires after the render is fully complete, onRenderReady(callback) fires after template nodes are initialized but before zones are processed, and onPreRender(callback) fires before the render starts. If the element has already rendered, all three execute the callback immediately.
rendering.promise: Each ArpaElement instance exposes a promise property that resolves when the render cycle completes. This allows external code to await the element's readiness with await element.promise.

extending.overview: ArpaElement is designed to be extended. Every Arpadroid component inherits from it and leverages its lifecycle hooks, configuration system, and utility methods to implement component-specific behavior.
extending.pattern: The standard extension pattern is: define a class extending ArpaElement (or a subclass), override getDefaultConfig() calling super.getDefaultConfig() with your defaults, override the needed lifecycle hooks, implement render() or provide a template in the config, and register the element with defineCustomElement('my-tag', MyElement).
extending.hooksToOverride: Common hooks to override: _preInitialize() for early setup, _initialize() for post-config initialization, initializeProperties() to validate or compute properties before first render, onReady() for async initialization, render() or _getTemplate() for content generation, _initializeNodes() for post-render DOM queries, _onDomReady() for DOM-dependent setup, _onComplete() for final post-render logic, update() for reactive attribute changes, and _onDestroy() for cleanup.
extending.registration: Call defineCustomElement(tagName, ElementClass) to register your component as a custom element. The tag name must contain a hyphen per the Custom Elements specification. ArpaElement itself is registered as arpa-element.
extending.bindMethod: Use this.bind('methodName') in your constructor or _initialize() to bind methods to the element instance, preserving the correct this context when methods are passed as callbacks.
